title: Vomit on Car Upholstery
timeEstimate: 30 minutes to several hours; car-specific constraints
stainChemistry: Same chemistry as vomit on home upholstery — protein + stomach acid (HCl) + bile salts + partially digested food — but car context changes the protocol in important ways: (1) car upholstery is usually synthetic (polyester / nylon) and built to be cleanable, (2) car seats have FOAM and SEAT SPRINGS underneath that absorb liquid and hold odor, (3) the car's HVAC system will pick up lingering odor molecules and re-distribute them through the cabin for months, (4) car upholstery can't be removed easily to wash separately, and (5) you can't fully dry a car interior the way you can a couch — trapped moisture in foam or under carpet backing leads to persistent odor + mold. Fast response + aggressive extraction + ventilation + enzyme treatment + odor-eliminator (ozone or pro-grade enzyme dwell) are the car-specific adjustments.
whyThisWorks: Solids removal + cold acid-neutralization + enzyme protein digestion + Dawn fat emulsion + vinegar rinse handles the chemistry. Aggressive weighted extraction prevents foam saturation from becoming permanent. Baking soda + sun-in-open-doors dries moisture and absorbs residual odor. HVAC-running-on-fresh-air-only while treating pulls odor out rather than through the vents. Pro-escalation threshold is lower than home upholstery because car foam + HVAC mean odor can linger for months if not caught cleanly.
defaultAssumption: Kid car-sick, adult car-sick (e.g., after a night out), pet car-sick, or morning sickness. Fresh + caught at a gas station = real home chance with aggressive extraction. Dried / overnight / baked in the sun (summer car + vomit = worst case) = pro auto detailer with ozone. Leather seats = different protocol entirely (leather-specific card, generally wipe-clean + conditioning).

spottingProtocol.equipment: N95 mask, disposable gloves, garbage bag, parking spot
spottingProtocol.instruction: Pull over / park as fast as safely possible if you're driving. Gloves + mask. Open ALL doors. Turn car HVAC OFF (or to recirculate OFF + fresh air IN) — you don't want vomit odor cycling through the vent system. If in hot weather, parking in shade matters — heat sets protein. If it's a pet accident, mask especially.

spottingProtocol.technique: Spoon / dull edge into sealed garbage bag
spottingProtocol.equipment: Spoon, plastic card, garbage bag, paper towels
spottingProtocol.instruction: Scoop solids with a spoon or plastic card into a sealed garbage bag. Press paper towels STRAIGHT DOWN on liquid, lift, repeat. Don't rub or wipe — drives liquid into foam. Work outside-in. Seal and discard waste outside the car.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cloth, dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen a clean white cloth with cold water. Press firmly on the spot, lift, rotate, repeat. Cold dilutes stomach acid before it damages dye. Weighted dry towel 5 min after blotting to pull moisture back up. Never pour — car upholstery foam is unforgiving about saturation.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Enzyme pretreat (Nature's Miracle / OxiClean Versatile / Biz) in warm water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat in WARM water (bath temperature, under 100°F — hot water sets protein). Dampen a cloth, press on the spot, cover with a second damp towel to keep enzyme wet. 15–20 min. Don't use hot.
Work Dawn in cool water into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Lifts bile and fat. Damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.equipment: White vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dilute white vinegar on damp cloth, 5 min. Neutralizes residual acid and VOCs causing lingering odor. The car-specific version of the step most guides skip. Blot.

spottingProtocol.agent: Weighted Dry Towel + Baking Soda Overnight
spottingProtocol.equipment: Heavy dry towels + weight, baking soda
spottingProtocol.instruction: Stack dry towels, weight heavily, 30 min. Swap for fresh dry towels, repeat 2–3 times. Then dust baking soda generously over the area. Leave overnight with all doors / windows open (shade, not sun). Vacuum baking soda next day with car vacuum attachment.

spottingProtocol.technique: Dry the foam completely + vent residual odor
spottingProtocol.equipment: Parked car with all doors open, sunlight nearby but not baking interior
spottingProtocol.instruction: Park in a well-ventilated spot, open all doors for several hours. Sunlight near the car helps UV kill residual bacteria; direct sun baking the inside = don't if hot outside (over ~80°F) — heat can set any residual protein.

spottingProtocol.agent: Odor Check + Escalate to Auto Detailer With Ozone
spottingProtocol.equipment: Nose, auto detailer phone if needed
spottingProtocol.instruction: Drive the car for a day with HVAC on FRESH AIR (not recirculate). Smell-check. Lingering odor = foam is still contaminated. Auto detailer with OZONE treatment (car sealed + ozone generator runs, humans + pets out) is the car-specific pro option that home can't replicate.

steps: 1. Park + open doors + HVAC OFF. Gloves + mask.
2. Scrape solids into sealed bag. Blot liquid straight down.
6. White vinegar + cool water damp cloth rinse.
7. Weighted dry towels × 2–3 rounds + baking soda overnight.
8. Open-door ventilation + mild sunlight next day.
9. Residual odor = auto detailer with ozone.
products: Enzyme pretreat (Nature's Miracle / OxiClean Versatile / Biz)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool to warm (<100°F / 38°C) — never hot, never steam
warnings: NEVER hot water or steam — sets protein
NEVER pour liquid on car upholstery — foam saturation is permanent territory
Car HVAC picks up odor — run fresh air ONLY during and after
Hot parked car + residual vomit = protein set permanently
Pro escalation threshold is LOW on car upholstery — ozone is the car-specific tool

homeSolutions.agent: Pull Over and Don't Drive With It in the Car
homeSolutions.instruction: If you're driving when it happens, pull over as soon as safely possible. Driving the vomit around heats the seat (engine heat, sun through windshield) and drives odor into the HVAC system. Park in shade if summer. Open ALL doors. Turn the HVAC OFF completely — you don't want vents cycling vomit odor through the cabin. Gloves and a mask on before you touch anything. Keep a trash bag ready.

homeSolutions.agent: Solid Removal — Scrape Into a Sealed Bag
homeSolutions.instruction: Spoon or plastic card to scoop solids into a garbage bag. Don't rub or wipe — that drives liquid into the seat foam. Press paper towels STRAIGHT DOWN on liquid, lift straight up, press fresh ones. Keep going until no more liquid transfers. Seal the garbage bag and put it OUTSIDE the car immediately.

Dampen a clean white cloth with cold water, press firmly on the spot. Lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. Cold water dilutes the stomach acid before it damages the seat dye. Keep blotting until no more color transfers. NEVER pour water on a car seat — car seats have foam underneath, and saturated foam dries slowly and holds odor for months.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat in WARM (Not Hot) Water
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat — Nature's Miracle, OxiClean Versatile, or Biz — in WARM water. Bath temperature, under 100°F. Not hot. Hot water sets the protein. Dampen a white cloth in the enzyme solution, press on the spot, cover with a second damp towel to keep it wet, wait 15–20 minutes. The enzyme digests the protein + food residue.
Work a drop of Dawn in cool water into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Dawn emulsifies the fat and bile salts in vomit. Damp-cloth rinse — don't pour.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar + Water Rinse for Acid + Odor
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix equal parts white vinegar + cool water. Dab on a damp cloth, press on the spot 5 minutes. Neutralizes residual stomach acid and breaks down the volatile compounds that cause persistent smell. This is the step that separates "looks clean, smells bad" from actually clean.

Stack heavy dry towels on the spot, weight them with a book or bag of sand, 30 minutes. Swap for fresh dry towels, repeat 2–3 times. Then dust baking soda generously over the area. Leave overnight with ALL DOORS OPEN (shade, not sun). Vacuum the baking soda next morning with a car-vac attachment. Aggressive extraction matters more on car seats than aggressive cleaning — foam that stays wet = mold + odor for months.

Park in a well-ventilated shade spot the next day with all doors open for several hours. Mild sunlight near the car helps. Avoid direct sun baking the interior on a hot day — over 80°F + vomit residue = protein set permanently. Don't use HVAC while treating; when you drive, run fresh air ONLY, not recirculate, for the next several days.

homeSolutions.agent: Smell Check + Pro Ozone for Lingering Odor
homeSolutions.instruction: Drive for a day with HVAC on fresh air. If you can still smell vomit (especially as the car heats up in sunlight), the foam is still contaminated and home chemistry is at its ceiling. Call an AUTO DETAILER with OZONE treatment — they seal the car with an ozone generator inside (no people / pets during treatment) and oxidize the last residual VOCs. Tell them: "Vomit on car upholstery, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated with cold + enzyme (warm, not hot) + Dawn + vinegar + baking soda + open-door dry. [Odor persists / foam saturation suspected]. Need ozone treatment."

steps: 1. Pull over. Open doors. HVAC OFF. PPE.
2. Scrape solids into sealed bag outside car. Blot straight down.
4. Enzyme pretreat WARM (not hot) water, 15–20 min.
6. White vinegar + water rinse for acid + odor.
7. Weighted dry towels × 2–3 + baking soda overnight with doors open.
8. Ventilate + mild sunlight next day; fresh-air HVAC only.
9. Odor lingers = auto detailer with ozone.
products: Enzyme pretreat (Nature's Miracle / OxiClean Versatile / Biz)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if odor persists after a full cycle + ventilation, if the vomit baked in a hot car before treatment (protein likely set), or if the seat material is leather / Alcantara (different protocol).
warnings: Biohazard — PPE + sealed disposal outside car
NEVER hot water or steam — sets protein
NEVER pour — foam saturation is persistent-odor territory
Car HVAC redistributes odor — fresh-air only
Never bake the car in hot sun with vomit residue
Pro ozone threshold is LOW — car foam holds odor

neverDo: Never hot water or steam — sets protein
Never pour liquid on car upholstery
Never drive far with vomit in the car (heat + HVAC)
Never run HVAC on recirculate during or after
fiberSensitivities: Polyester / nylon car fabric: tolerates full protocol
Leather: different protocol — don't use this card
Alcantara / suede-like: pro — most DIY chemistry damages the nap
Vinyl: tolerates mild cleaning but test first

materialWarnings: Car-specific: HVAC + foam + heat combine to make odor permanent
Pro ozone is the car-specific escalation
Baking in hot sun = protein set

escalation.when: Odor persists after a full home cycle, vomit baked in a hot sun-parked car before treatment, seat is leather / Alcantara / suede, or HVAC smells like vomit when running.
escalation.whatToTell: "Vomit on car upholstery, seat type [polyester / leather / Alcantara], age [fresh / overnight / older / baked in heat], home-treated with cold + enzyme (warm, not hot) + Dawn + vinegar + baking soda + ventilation. No hot water / steam. [Odor persists / HVAC contaminated / foam saturation suspected]. Need ozone treatment."
escalation.specialistType: auto detailer with enzyme cleaning + ozone generator capability

contextQuestions: How long ago — was it driven around before treatment?
Has the car been parked in hot sun since the incident?
Seat type — polyester / leather / Alcantara / vinyl?
Has HVAC been running? Recirculate or fresh air?
Adult / kid / pet? (pet vomit has different bile content)
Visible staining or just odor now?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: The big car-specific variables: heat exposure (hot parked car) sets protein fast, HVAC on recirculate redistributes odor through the whole cabin for months, and foam under the seat cover is the real ceiling for home chemistry — once saturated, ozone treatment is often the only clean fix. Baby car seat covers are often removable and machine-washable — check before in-place treatment.
